steps.title: Spot the two-part structure
steps.content: The passage has two halves: it opens with a statement about a common criticism (franchise movies “are often criticized for being predictable”), then spends the rest of the passage responding to that criticism.

steps.title: Apply this structure to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D correctly identifies the structure: criticism followed by counter-argument.

The passage develops the counter-argument through concession (acknowledging Spider-Man's similarities to other Marvel films), generalization (franchise movies “are rarely as stale as some people claim”), and counterexample (Logan's originality).

Choices A, B, and C misidentify it completely. That matches choice D — D is correct.
